title: Adapt ffmpeg audio recorder for session-triggered start/stop
description: Port the meeting-recorder ffmpeg script into a session-aware recorder. Must: auto-detect USB mic device name via 'ffmpeg -list_devices true -f dshow -i dummy', start recording when session starts (S3 polling), stop on session end or 'stop audio' command, output WAV 44100Hz stereo to session folder, handle graceful SIGINT for clean stop. No interactive prompts. Config via environment variables (AUDIO_DEVICE, SESSION_POLL_URL).

substeps.title: USB mic auto-detection via ffmpeg dshow
substeps.notes: audio/lib/device-detect.js — scores devices by keyword, AUDIO_DEVICE override

substeps.title: S3 polling for session start/stop events
substeps.notes: audio/lib/session-poller.js — EventEmitter, polls metadata.json + stop-audio command

substeps.title: Core ffmpeg recorder with start/stop/SIGINT
substeps.notes: audio/lib/ffmpeg-recorder.js — 44100Hz stereo WAV, graceful q-stop with SIGTERM fallback

tests: Run with no USB mic -> error message with list of available devices
Run with valid device -> WAV file created, 44100Hz stereo verified
Send stop signal -> recording stops cleanly, file is valid (not corrupted)
10-minute recording -> file size reasonable (~100MB WAV), no gaps
Auto-detect picks correct USB mic when multiple audio devices exist
research_notes: ffmpeg DirectShow on Windows: use '-f dshow -list_devices true -i dummy' to enumerate. Device names contain 'Microphone', 'USB', 'Wireless'. S3 polling reads sessions/<session_id>/metadata.json; start on status=active, stop on status=completed or ended_at set. Output: WAV 44100Hz stereo (-ar 44100 -ac 2). SIGINT handler must kill ffmpeg child process cleanly — ffmpeg accepts 'q' on stdin or SIGINT/SIGTERM which flushes buffers before exit.
